perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated, neither you nor your representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose. In any event, neither you nor any of your representatives will use the Evaluation Materials in any way which is or may be detrimental to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated, neither you nor your representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose, except for internal analysis. In any event, neither you nor any of your representatives will use the Evaluation Materials in any way which is or may be detrimental to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'except for internal analysis' after a possible transaction is not consummated creates an in-text contradiction. It initially states that the Evaluation Materials cannot be used for any purpose if the transaction fails, however by adding the exception the contract provides a way for using it. This could lead to disputes about the limits of 'internal analysis' and whether it is considered 'detrimental' to the Company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, affiliates, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), and lenders (collectively, “your representatives”) who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you, and shall each expressly agree (in writing, if requested by the Company or its advisors), to treat such information confidential in accordance with the agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated, neither you nor your representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose, except for internal analysis. In any event, neither you nor any of your representatives will use the Evaluation Materials in any way which is or may be detrimental to the Company. You will be responsible for any breach of this agreement by you or your representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Evaluation Materials cannot be used for any purpose if the transaction is not consummated. The changed text adds 'except for internal analysis,' which directly contradicts the original restriction. The contradicted text contains both the original statement and the changed text. The addition creates an exception where previously there was none, leading to a direct conflict in the document's instructions regarding the use of Evaluation Materials after a failed transaction. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Accordingly, unless required by applicable law or regulatory authority, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person (including, but not limited to, any customer, employee, supplier, creditor or competitor of the Company) the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof, nor will you or any of your representatives make inquiry about the Company’s business to any customer, employee, supplier, creditor or competitor of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Accordingly, unless required by applicable law or regulatory authority, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person (including, but not limited to, any customer, employee, supplier, creditor or competitor of the Company) the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof, nor will you or any of your representatives make inquiry about the Company’s business to any customer of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The modification removes the prohibition on making inquiries about the Company's business to 'employee, supplier, creditor or competitor of the Company'. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the paragraph initially aims to prevent any disclosures regarding the transaction but now allows direct inquiries with employees, suppliers, creditors, and competitors, thereby undermining the original intent of the clause. These inquiries could reveal the transaction is being made, thereby contradicting the purpose of the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The disclosure of your possible interest in purchasing the Company could have a material adverse effect on the Company’s business if for any reason an agreement of purchase and sale is not consummated or a disclosure is made prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction. Accordingly, unless required by applicable law or regulatory authority, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person (including, but not limited to, any customer, employee, supplier, creditor or competitor of the Company) the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof, nor will you or any of your representatives make inquiry about the Company’s business to any customer of the Company. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, you further agree that, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, directly or indirectly, enter into any agreement, arrangement or understanding or any discussions that any lead to such agreement, arrangement or understanding with any person (other than the Company) regarding a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited inquiries to customers, employees, suppliers, creditors, or competitors. The changed text removes the prohibition on inquiries to employees, suppliers, creditors, and competitors. The contradicted text states that 'The disclosure of your possible interest in purchasing the Company could have a material adverse effect on the Company’s business'. Because the changed text allows inquiries to employees, suppliers, creditors, and competitors of the Company, this opens up the possibility of those inquiries revealing the intent to purchase the company, which would then trigger a material adverse effect on the Company's business, directly contradicting the goal stated in the contradicted text. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists because the change directly undermines the original intent.

perturbation.original_text: You also agree not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in a Possible Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition by you and such third parry without the prior written consent of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: You also agree not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in a Possible Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition by you and such third party, except for your affiliates, without the prior written consent of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The inclusion of 'except for your affiliates' creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, no discussions about equity participation or joint acquisition are allowed with third parties without written consent. The exception allows those discussions with affiliates, which potentially violates the confidentiality provisions. The term of affiliate is previously defined, thus the use of affiliate creates a loop hole in the original texts of section 'No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the course of your evaluation, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to Mesirow or employees or representatives of the Company specified by Mesirow. Accordingly, you and your representatives agree not to directly or indirectly contact or communicate with any executive or other employee of the Company concerning a Possible Transaction, or to seek any information in connection therewith from such person, without the express written consent of Mesirow. You also agree not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in a Possible Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition by you and such third party, except for your affiliates, without the prior written consent of the Company. Without the Company’s prior written consent, neither you nor any of your affiliates will for a period of two years from the date of this agreement directly or indirectly solicit for employment or employ any of the officers or employees of the Company (or any person whose activities are dedicated to the Company).
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited any discussion with third parties regarding equity participation without the Company's consent. The changed text introduces an exception for 'affiliates'. The contradicted text also includes the statement from the original text. The change introduces an exception that wasn't there before. As the explanation indicates, this creates a loop hole because 'affiliates' are previously defined. This creates an inconsistency with the overarching restrictions detailed in the contradicted text, indicating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that until two years from the date of this agreement, without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company, neither you nor any of your representatives will (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that until one year from the date of this agreement, without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company, neither you nor any of your representatives will (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'two years' to 'one year' regarding the standstill agreement contradicts with the 'Non-Disclosure' agreement, which is in effect for two years. If the standstill is in effect for only one year, but the non-disclosure remains for two, then they are obligated not to disclose for two years, but can acquire securities or make proposals after one year, which could force the Company to make a public announcement (inconsistent with the terms).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The disclosure of your possible interest in purchasing the Company could have a material adverse effect on the Company’s business if for any reason an agreement of purchase and sale is not consummated or a disclosure is made prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction. Accordingly, unless required by applicable law or regulatory authority, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person (including, but not limited to, any customer, employee, supplier, creditor or competitor of the Company) the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof, nor will you or any of your representatives make inquiry about the Company’s business to any customer, employee, supplier, creditor or competitor of the Company. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, you further agree that, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, directly or indirectly, enter into any agreement, arrangement or understanding or any discussions that any lead to such agreement, arrangement or understanding with any person (other than the Company) regarding a Possible Transaction. The term “person” as used in this agreement shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corporation, the Company, governmental agency or body, stock exchange, partnership, limited liability company, association or individual. The terms of this section “Non-Disclosure” shall remain in effect for 2 years from the effective date of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a two-year standstill agreement, which aligns with the two-year non-disclosure agreement in the contradicted text. The changed text reduces the standstill period to one year. According to the explanation, this creates a contradiction because after one year, the party could acquire securities or make proposals, potentially forcing the company to make a public announcement, which would violate the two-year non-disclosure agreement. Therefore, the changed text creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, satisfying the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
